title: Original intent of the contact experiment
display_title: Original intent of the contact experiment
wikitext:
[]: Original intent of the contact experiment?             Quote Post by Yue » Mon Jul 11, 2011 12:10 am So we can assume from Gendo's attempts to salvage Yui, Ritsuko mentioning "the chamber of guf was empty" like it was something they learned, and the official rulings of the contact experiments as failures that the end result was not exactly what they planned. What IS planned, obviously they never quite say, but I think I worked the rough idea out. The original plan was probably not to insert human souls into the Evas, but to imbue them with their own souls, though Gherim didn't quite nail down the "how." I think what they expected to happen was that the "contact" being made was of a being with a soul to a being without one; the hope was that it would work like a lit torch lighting an unlit one, exposure to a working being with a soul would somehow imbue the Evas with their own souls (which might also mean they didn't originally intend for the kids to pilot these things; at least I HOPE they didn't). Whether it failed because, as Ritsuko said, there were no "freed up" souls to give out, or just because metaphysical biology doesn't work that way, I don't know. EDIT: Just occurred to me that Yui is a professional metaphysical biologist, so we can assume she'd know whether or not this would work from the start. Maybe the real question is whether she told anyone else?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Jul 11, 2011 7:58 am Yui's intent was certainly to escape her physical form and become Unit 01, and she tells Fuyutsuki as much in ep21'. On her part it's a logical move; her views are heretical to SEELE and they probably want to get rid of her as soon as possible, so she puts herself in a position where they can't afford to destroy her. But of course Naoko and the rest of Gehirn (even Gendou?) aren't aware of this plan, so they must have been expecting something else, which is where I think your idea is about right: by sending a living soul into Eva, they hoped to awaken its own soul. It makes sense: one's soul is defined by one's interactions with others, so introduce an other and you'll get a soul, right? But that didn't happen, either because Yui didn't allow it to or (more likely) because souls can't actually be created or destroyed in Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jul 11, 2011 9:13 am staying in EVA was pretty much Yui's own, secret agenda, so the official goal must've been something else, right. But I think before we start some convoluted fanwanking, we should take the most local path: She was probably meant to pilot it. Only kids work and artifical beings don't have souls/need to be given some in the EVA universe, but yeah, they probably didn't know that yet. Or most likely, Yui worked it out (since she brought Shinji along - she expected him to become the Chosen One) but didn't tell anyone.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Jul 11, 2011 10:08 am So if i got it right, the theory presented is that Gehirn hoped to awaken a possible proto-soul inside the Eva via contact with an already formed, existing soul? Interesting, though i think that... Kendrix wrote: She was probably meant to pilot it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jul 11, 2011 10:24 am I think the original intent, as in, the way the whole idea of Evas was sold to the powers that be in the first place, was that the intended pilot would make contact with the Eva and imbue it with an imprint/copy of the pilot's own soul; thus, when the pilot needs to synchronize with Eva to make it work he/she's syncing with his/her own soul (the Dummy System in reverse, basically). After Yui's disastrous (but quite intentional, though no one but Fuyu knew this at the time) attempt Kyoko tried to refine the process, which resulted in her soul being divided instead of imprinting properly as intended. Alternately, the intent might have been a controlled soul division; they knew this was possible due to Lilith/Rei/Unit-00 shenanegins, so they might have reasoned that it was an acceptable sacrifice. Yui (apparently, but not really) botched it, Kyoko tried to get it right, and they gave up after that (though who knows what happened with Units 3 and 4).

[]:             Quote Post by Yue » Mon Jul 11, 2011 10:41 am Azathoth wrote: ? But that didn't happen, either because Yui didn't allow it to or (more likely) because souls can't actually be created or destroyed in Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Jul 11, 2011 10:56 am Bagheera wrote: I think the original intent, as in, the way the whole idea of Evas was sold to the powers that be in the first place, was that the intended pilot would make contact with the Eva and imbue it with an imprint/copy of the pilot's own soul; thus, when the pilot needs to synchronize with Eva to make it work he/she's syncing with his/her own soul (the Dummy System in reverse, basically). After Yui's disastrous (but quite intentional, though no one but Fuyu knew this at the time) attempt Kyoko tried to refine the process, which resulted in her soul being divided instead of imprinting properly as intended.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jul 11, 2011 11:09 am SaltyJoe wrote: There is one thing seriously bugging me about this theory: Eva-02 was meant to be deployed in frontline combat from day one. Why use a sceintist as it's primary piloting candidate then? And for that matter, why use the head of the project, presumably the most valuable player in the whole deal (they already had on instance of a disaster, so refined process or no, it's still very risky)? Wouldn't it be more logical to prepare some army schmo to be the "personality donor" for this whole affair, who not only is more likely to be effective in actual combat, but whose potentail loss wouldn't really impair Project E in the future?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Jul 11, 2011 11:43 am @ Yue: I think you are probably combining point A with Point B instead of moving from Point A to Point B. Yes, I've always felt that Ritsuko meant that they had originally planned to use unborn souls from the Guf in the Evas, but when the cupboard turned out to be bare, so to speak, they were forced to use salvaged souls. That Yui is a subject of an experiment known to imbue Eva with a human soul indicates to me that they had already learned at this point that there were no souls to be had in the Chamber of Guf and had moved on to salvaging human souls into the Evas. i.e. Salvaging a soul from the Chamber would probably have used some proceedure other than a Contact Experiment. This leaves unanswered the question of what did everyone else think would happen. (Yui planned to be absorbed completely. [url]http://wiki.evageeks.org/Theory_and_Analysis:Yui%27s_Agenda[/url]) Some possibilities: They thought she would only lose a portion of her soul, as happened subsequently with Kyoko. They were trying to copy her soul into Unit-01. @Bagheera: While it's possible that they planned to make Yui and Kyoko pilots, I think that that's probably a bit of a streach. It's more likely that the children were still the intended pilots, even if they would have just been synching with a copy or fragment. But perhaps you could develop this some more by tying it into Misato's presence at Second Impact. Was the experiment that gave them Kaworu actually supposed to create an Eva with Dr. Katsuragi or Misato as the pilot? (With Katsuragi's soul?) BTW, the "Soul Copy" theory used to be very popular before soul splitting was confirmed by the C.I. The theory was that Kyoko went insane because she somehow accidently ended up with the copy while Unit-02 got the real soul. (This was undercut by the apparent presense of insane Kyoko in Unit-02.) After soul-splitting became canon rather than theory, the notion that Kyoko's soul was, in fact, copied was rejected. An interesting question is: what was the plan if they had succeeded in snaging some souls from the Guf? Did they think that anyone would be able to synch with a soul that had never developed any ties? The objection that really important people wouldn't put themselves in danger is old and fruitless. Kirk and Spock shouldn't have explored the dangerous new planet themselves every week. What are those 400 crewmen for? But they did it anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jul 11, 2011 11:45 am Yue wrote: It couldn't be the first one, because the same thing happened in Naoko's contact experiment

[]:             Quote Post by Yue » Mon Jul 11, 2011 12:44 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Naoko? Are you thinking of this? http://wiki.evageeks.org/Theory_and_Analysis:Eva-00%27s_Soul A little confused, so just clarifying.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jul 14, 2011 2:01 pm Yes, Yui's a valuable scientist etc, but they were aware that the contact experiment might get dangerous, and they couldn't just have some random innocent person test it. Yui built it, so it'sd only logical for her to bear the consequences if stuff goes awry... Remember that flashing text from eps 16/20 suggest that Gendo was put on trial for what happened to Yui (probably since he was the highest ranked person overseeing the experiment), and there were newspaper articles/plenty of ppl talking about it where Gendo's own son could hear it. Imagine the outrage if they had gotten some low-ranked dude and treated him as expendable... - Must've been tough for poor Gendo, First, his wife "dies", and then, they accuse him of murdering her....

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jul 14, 2011 3:45 pm I think they expected her to possess it only temporary, like some sort of new-age mind projection deal, with her body held safely inside until she was done with the test. either that or some sort of ghost-dubbing scheme strait out of GitS 2.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 20, 2011 10:48 pm @Original topic: CI wrote: A copy of a personality is made in the dummy plug, but a soul cannot be copied. Why can't it be done, or how would it be done? As far as the technology branches of Seele and Nerv are concerned, it is a theme of research integral to seeking out the Path to God, and study into how to deal with the problem has spanned many years. Examples of failure include Yui and Asuka's mother. The dummy plug is something that came about in the midst of that research.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Jul 21, 2011 9:01 am Reichu wrote: The Evas would effectively have newborn souls, right? In that case, perhaps they'd start developing ties with whomever was thrown into the entry plug.

Article: Original intent of the contact experiment
 with the copy while Unit-02 got the real soul. (This was undercut by the apparent presense of insane Kyoko in Unit-02.)
